treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 12.5 mg/m2/day initially, later adjusted to 10 mg/m2/day
treatment_information.treatment_response: Improved energy levels, normalized ACTH levels, but experienced several hypoglycemic episodes and seizures during illness

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 15 mg of glucose gel every 15 to 30 minutes as needed to maintain blood glucose levels >60 mg/dL
treatment_information.treatment_response: Effective in treating hypoglycemia, no more episodes of hypoglycemia recorded after age 9
Able to tolerate a soft diet
Symptoms stable with no report of corneal ulcerations

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Dietary supplement containing magnesium, riboflavin, and puracol (feverfew)

treatment_timeline.event: Initial presentation with altered mental status due to hypoglycemia, diagnosed with primary adrenal insufficiency
Started on maintenance hydrocortisone therapy (12.5 mg/m2/day)
Experienced hypoglycemic episodes leading to seizures during viral illness
No more severe hypoglycemia (<50 mg/dL) occurred, but glucose levels were low (50 to 60 mg/dL) during acute illness
Notable weight gain, concerns for development of metabolic syndrome
Developed severe dysmotility, confirmed by esophagram and manometry study
Required balloon dilation again for achalasia

patient_outcome.final_status: Stable with normal puberty development and regular menstrual cycles
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Regular monitoring of growth, puberty, and development
patient_outcome.complications: Hypoglycemic episodes, seizures, weight gain, elevated hemoglobin A1c levels, dysmotility, migraines
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: No more episodes of hypoglycemia recorded after age 9
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Able to tolerate a soft diet, headaches under control, mild learning difficulty but able to maintain average grades
